problem.title: Minimum Depth of Binary Tree
problem.description: Given a binary tree, find its minimum depth. The minimum depth is the number of nodes along the shortest path from the root node down to the nearest leaf node.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – BFS
solutions.description: Use BFS (level-order traversal) and return the depth of the first leaf node encountered. Because BFS explores nodes level by level, the very first leaf found is guaranteed to be at minimum depth. This is more efficient than DFS for wide trees because BFS stops as soon as the first leaf is reached.
